Package org.neuroml1.model.channel

Class ChannelType

  • java.lang.Object
    • org.neuroml1.model.channel.ChannelType
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    Cloneable, org.jvnet.jaxb2_commons.lang.CopyTo2, org.jvnet.jaxb2_commons.lang.Equals2, org.jvnet.jaxb2_commons.lang.HashCode2, org.jvnet.jaxb2_commons.lang.ToString2
    public class ChannelType
extends Object
implements Cloneable, org.jvnet.jaxb2_commons.lang.CopyTo2, org.jvnet.jaxb2_commons.lang.Equals2, org.jvnet.jaxb2_commons.lang.HashCode2, org.jvnet.jaxb2_commons.lang.ToString2
    Definition of a voltage/concentration dependent cell membrane conductance

    Java class for ChannelType complex type.

    The following schema fragment specifies the expected content contained within this class. 

     <complexType name="ChannelType">
   <complexContent>
     <restriction base="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}anyType">
       <sequence>
         <element name="status" type="{http://morphml.org/metadata/schema}Status" minOccurs="0"/>
         <group ref="{http://morphml.org/metadata/schema}metadata"/>
         <group ref="{http://morphml.org/metadata/schema}referencedata"/>
         <element name="parameters" type="{http://morphml.org/channelml/schema}Parameters" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="current_voltage_relation" type="{http://morphml.org/channelml/schema}CurrentVoltageRelation"/>
         <element name="hh_gate" type="{http://morphml.org/channelml/schema}Deprecated_HHGate" maxOccurs="unbounded" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="ks_gate" type="{http://morphml.org/channelml/schema}Deprecated_KSGate" maxOccurs="unbounded" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="impl_prefs" type="{http://morphml.org/channelml/schema}ImplementationPrefs" minOccurs="0"/>
       </sequence>
       <attribute name="name" use="required" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string" />
       <attribute name="density" type="{http://morphml.org/metadata/schema}YesNo" default="yes" />
     </restriction>
   </complexContent>
 </complexType>

      • getGroup

        public List<String> getGroup()
        Gets the value of the group property.

        This accessor method returns a reference to the live list, not a snapshot. Therefore any modification you make to the returned list will be present inside the JAXB object. This is why there is not a set method for the group property.

        For example, to add a new item, do as follows: 

            getGroup().add(newItem);

        Objects of the following type(s) are allowed in the list String

        Gets the value of the authorList property.

        public List<Publication> getPublication()

        This accessor method returns a reference to the live list, not a snapshot. Therefore any modification you make to the returned list will be present inside the JAXB object. This is why there is not a set method for the publication property.

        For example, to add a new item, do as follows: 

            getPublication().add(newItem);

        Objects of the following type(s) are allowed in the list Publication
